Welcome back guys, this is Themeplate10, nicknamed the Aladdin, and this is what you're going to be using as your minimum viable funnel version of your launch email. So this is if you don't have a webinar, you don't have a sales video, you don't have a sales page, you don't have time or budget or whatever to create any of those, and you want to really just link your email to a buy button. So you'll need to spice up this version of the anti launch email with just those few extra ingredients that allow it to stand alone without a sales page. So if you're going to roll with this one, I recommend also checking out modules six and seven as we borrow elements from the sales and closing subsequence to really make this one tick. But the main thing we're using here is what I call the magic carpet ride method to give your prospect that vivid walkthrough of your program's main features and benefits in an evocative way that really allows them to project themselves for it to future pace it and see themselves participating in those outcomes that your modules and lessons and features and benefits deliver. So the point is not to make this email about consuming information about your offer. We do need to communicate that information, but we don't want it to be about consuming it. We want them to consume the vision of what life will look like using your program, which accomplishes both. You know, it turns that information and alchemizes it into a consumable vision. So all that said, here is the theme plate. So you will open up with the offer now live moment. You've had your pre launch, so it is appropriate to open with that link. Don't call it a launch high level outcomes. They will achieve and then you'll dive into that Rome is burning moment like we spoke about before, which could include that moment of highest tension. Why it's not a launch for you. So this is where you add that credibility proof testimonials, case studies, and then unlike the anti launch launch here, you will do a program walkthrough and stack the value, you know, before getting into your offer, which would also include any surface level urgency or scarcity you're using. You know, if you are closing your cart later, you know, in two days and three days and five days, whatever it is, this is where you would mention it. And then you would challenge your prospect to achieve that quick, valuable win. And you will position against the alternative, you know, the cost of going backwards.
